mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuations and driving negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based demand contraction, reducing corporate earnings expectations and driving negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with accuracy below 50%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that supports corporate margins, generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during tightening periods with Rank IC below 0.03, or returns turn negative when monthly INDPRO growth exceeds 1%.

mechanism_narrative: High policy rates combined with declining nonfarm payrolls create stagflationary pressure that forces equity repricing, resulting in negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high rates and falling payrolls yields positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth signals robust economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, driving immediate positive equity market momentum within one week.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth correlates with negative 5-day forward returns in >55% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation readings above 320 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels above 320 fail to predict negative 60-day returns with accuracy below 45%, or the signal reverses to positive during high inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling industrial production indicates a supply-demand mismatch that depresses earnings forecasts, driving negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern yields positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances, or the interaction term shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample tests.
